features.summary: Goldman Sachs reported strong Q4 2024 results with revenues of $13.9 billion, EPS of $11.95, and ROE of 14.6%. Full year revenues grew 16% to $53.5 billion with EPS increasing 77% to $40.54. The firm maintained its #1 M&A advisory position and achieved record financing revenues of $9.1 billion. Asset & Wealth Management reached record assets under supervision of $3.1 trillion with management fees exceeding $10 billion. The company announced formation of a Capital Solutions Group to better serve clients across public and private markets.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed strong optimism for 2025, citing increased CEO confidence post-election, robust investment banking backlogs, and improved regulatory backdrop. They expect continued pickup in M&A and IPO activity throughout 2025, with sponsors showing increased appetite for dealmaking. The firm is targeting mid-teens ROE through improved Asset & Wealth Management returns and elimination of Platform Solutions drag. Management anticipates high-single digit growth in AWM management fees and progress toward $1 billion annual incentive fee target.
features.risks: Key risks include potential policy uncertainty from the new administration across immigration, trade, tax, and energy policies that could shift market sentiment. Geopolitical risks and cyber threats remain ongoing concerns. Interest rate movements could impact the constructive environment, and the firm acknowledges that market conditions can change rapidly. Regulatory uncertainty persists despite expectations for a more favorable environment, and the firm is engaged in litigation against the Federal Reserve regarding stress testing transparency.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include the structural shift toward private credit and alternative asset classes, where Goldman is uniquely positioned at the intersection of public and private markets. The new Capital Solutions Group organization aims to capture more financing opportunities. Continued scaling of the alternatives platform with $72 billion in fundraising and deployment of raised capital should drive performance fees. Operating efficiency initiatives through AI, automation, and organizational optimization are expected to create capacity for reinvestment while improving margins.
